basic_details.job_title: IIT Guwahati Sports Instructors Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 26 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati (IIT Guwahati)
basic_details.short_information: The latest Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati (IIT Guwahati) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Contractual Sports Instructors posts! Published on the official website iitg.ac.in, this recruitment drive offers 26 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants up to 45 years of age and possessing a Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education (B.P.Ed), Diploma/Certificate in Coaching, 10+2 with Certificate/Diploma in Fitness Training, or a Valid Lifeguard Certificate can participate in the walk-in recruitment process on 24 June 2026. Find all details about the IIT Guwahati Contractual Sports

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 45 years for all posts.

qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education (B.P.Ed), Diploma/Certificate in coaching, 10+2 with Certificate/Diploma in fitness training, Valid lifeguard certificate
qualification.details: Athletics, Badminton, Basketball, Cricket, Chess, Football, Hockey, Lawn Tennis, Squash, Swimming, Table Tennis, Volleyball, Weightlifting, Minor Game: Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education (B.P.Ed) or any graduate with a Diploma/Certificate in coaching from a recognized university/participation in state/university-level sports competitions. Water Polo: Candidate must be a water polo player either in district/ state/ university/ national/ international level competition with 10+2/Certificate/Diploma in coaching/ Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education (B.P.Ed) from a recognized university. Gymnasium: Bachelor Degree in Physical Education (B.P.Ed) from a recognized university or 10 +2 passed with Certificate/Diploma in fitness training from any recognized institute. Lifeguard: Must have a valid lifeguard certificate.

all_official_links.label: Download IIT Guwahati Job 2026 Notification PDF

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for IIT Guwahati Contractual Sports Instructors?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview for the Contractual Sports Instructors posts at IIT Guwahati is scheduled for 24 June 2026. Candidates should ensure they reach the venue on time.

faq.question: Q2. What is the total number of vacancies announced by IIT Guwahati?
faq.answer: IIT Guwahati has announced a total of 26 vacancies for the Contractual Sports Instructors positions across various sports disciplines.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the Contractual Sports Instructors posts?
faq.answer: The educational qualifications vary by post but generally include a Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education (B.P.Ed), Diploma/Certificate in coaching, or relevant certifications like fitness training or lifeguard certificates.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for applying to these positions?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all the Contractual Sports Instructors posts is 45 years.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Contractual Sports Instructors positions?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 35,000 per month.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for the IIT Guwahati recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Contractual Sports Instructors posts?
faq.answer: The selection process will involve a Practical Test followed by an Interview.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the walk-in interview be conducted?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be conducted at Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati, Assam, Pin code: 781039.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should candidates carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original documents, one set of self-attested photocopies, and recent passport-size photographs.

faq.question: Q10. Is this a permanent job?
faq.answer: No, these are contractual positions. The job type is specified as Contractual.
